front: What is the primary focus of the Human Perspective in UI/UX design?
back: The Human Perspective focuses on the user's needs, cognitive abilities, limitations, and psychology. It prioritizes creating intuitive and accessible interfaces that align with how people think and work.

front: What is the main goal of the Implementation Perspective?
back: The Implementation Perspective is concerned with the technical feasibility of a design. It focuses on how the interface will be built, the technology used, and the constraints and opportunities provided by the development platform.

front: How does the Designer/Artist Perspective differ from the other two perspectives?
back: The Designer/Artist Perspective focuses on the aesthetic, creative, and emotional impact of the interface. It is concerned with visual design, branding, creating a pleasing experience, and communicating the right feel through look and style.

front: Why is it crucial to balance all three design perspectives (Human, Implementation, Designer)?
back: Balancing all three perspectives ensures the final product is not only usable and accessible for people (Human), but also technically feasible to build (Implementation), and visually appealing and on-brand (Designer). Ignoring one can lead to failure.

front: From which perspective would you ask the question: 'What technology stack will best bring this design to life?'
back: This question originates from the Implementation Perspective, as it deals with the technical execution and feasibility of the design concept.
